description_long: This is a single-piece axe with a robust, wedge-shaped body and a broad, curved blade. The surface is heavily patinated, showing a mottled green and brown coloration typical of aged bronze or copper alloys, with visible corrosion and pitting. The handle is integrated into the same material as the blade, tapering to a blunt end and lacking any separate grip or wrapping. The blade edge is wide and slightly convex, with a thick spine that transitions smoothly into the handle. There is a small rectangular label or patch near the end of the handle, and faint engraved text is visible on one side of the blade. The overall form is utilitarian, with no decorative embellishments, suggesting it is a functional tool rather than a ceremonial object.
description: A patinated, single-piece axe with a broad, curved blade and integrated handle, showing signs of age and corrosion. The surface is mottled green-brown with visible text and a small label.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the side, clearly showing the broad, curved blade and the full length of the handle with a small label near the end.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, highlighting the thickness of the blade and handle, and the wedge-like profile.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the axe is visible, showing engraved text on the blade and the overall curvature of the tool.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the other edge, emphasizing the rough, textured surface and the tapering handle.
attribute_handle_type: Integrated, straight, wedge-shaped handle made of the same metal as the blade, with a rectangular cross-section and no added grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, curved single-edge blade with a convex edge profile, thick spine, and no beard or poll; utilitarian with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Single-piece construction with blade and handle forged or cast as one unit.
